page: Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 16 dedicated vCPUs derived from 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, supported by a 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data positions the E32-16s_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory metrics. The instance achieved a stress-ng single-core score of 1898.99 and a multi-core score of 15532.59. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 834.85 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 58.66 GB/sec. In database operations, the server processed 16.01 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 7995.58 on PassMark database operations. For language model workloads, it demonstrated strong performance in text generation with a small 135M model at 321.0 tokens/sec, while larger models like the 7B model achieved 15.6 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional compute scaling. However, the lack of local storage means users must provision external storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. The server is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers, and software compilation tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E32-16s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture and Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this memory-optimized instance offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It features no local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and contains no GPUs. Benchmark results indicate average performance across most categories, with a PassMark CPU score of 21320.68 and a Redis SET throughput of 16.01 million operations per second. It exhibits strong text generation capabilities on small language models, reaching 321.0 tokens per second. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including database operations, caching, and software compilation, where high memory capacity is prioritized over local storage.
